Masks are generated based on how much a:
Masks are generated based on how much a part of the image changed after denoising.
These masks are used to blend the original and final images together.
If the difference is low, the original pixels are used instead of the pixels returned by the inpainting process.

Skews whether partially masked image reg:
Skews whether partially masked image regions should be more likely to preserve the original content or favor inpainted content.
This may need to be adjusted depending on the Schedule bias, CFG Scale, prompt and Denoising strength.

Styles allow you to add custom text to p: Styles allow you to add custom text to prompt. Use the {prompt} token in style text, and it will be replaced with user's prompt when applying style. Otherwise, style's text will be added to the end of the prompt.

The blending strength of original conten:
The blending strength of original content is scaled proportionally with the decreasing noise level values at each step (sigmas).
This ensures that the influence of the denoiser and original content preservation is roughly balanced at each step.
This balance can be shifted using this parameter, controlling whether earlier or later steps have stronger preservation.

This page allows you to add some setting: This page allows you to add some settings to the main interface of txt2img and img2img tabs.

This parameter controls how much the mas: This parameter controls how much the mask should bias this sensitivity to difference.
This parameter controls how the original:
This parameter controls how the original latent vectors and denoised latent vectors are interpolated.
With higher values, the magnitude of the resulting blended vector will be closer to the maximum of the two interpolated vectors.
This can prevent the loss of contrast that occurs with linear interpolation.
This value represents the contrast betwe: This value represents the contrast between the opacity of the original and inpainted content.
This value represents the difference at: This value represents the difference at which the original pixels will have less than 50% opacity.

Toggle the Mode for updating resolution.
Resolution is always rounded to precision (default 64px).

⮃ = "Offset" updates both Width/Height from the average current resolution
⮅ = "One Dimension" changes only Width or Height
Toggle to lock the \"average\" width/hei:
Toggle to lock the "average" width/height values in the UI
It is recommended to "Lock" when switching between ARs in "Offset" mode.

🔒 = Locked
🔓 = Unlocked
